prestazioni: Engine swap to a Mitsubishi 4G63T (from the EVO I-III) with a forged internals build (JE 87mm pistons, Eagle H-beam rods, ACL race bearings) and a Garrett GT3076R turbo (0.82 A/R turbine housing) running 22 psi on E85 fuel. Custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust with a Tomei 80mm titanium muffler and a GReddy Profec B Spec II boost controller for precise tuning.

Upgraded drivetrain with a Mitsubishi Pajero V20 5-speed manual transmission (heavy-duty synchros) paired with a Cusco 2-way limited-slip differential (250 Nm torque capacity) and a Fidanza lightweight flywheel (11.5 lbs) for faster rev matching. Clutch Masters FX500 twin-disc clutch with a billet steel flywheel for durability under high torque loads.

Suspension overhaul with Ohlins TTX40 coilovers (remote reservoirs, 24-way adjustable damping) and custom poly bushings (SuperPro) for the leaf spring mounts. Front sway bar upgraded to a Whiteline 24mm adjustable unit, and rear leaf springs replaced with a custom 4-link setup (Currie Enterprises) for improved articulation and stability. Wilwood 6-piston calipers with 330mm rotors up front and 2-piston calipers with 316mm rotors in the rear, paired with Ferodo DS2500 brake pads.
estetica: Custom widebody conversion using a Liberty Walk-inspired kit (hand-fabricated steel fenders, 3-inch flares) to accommodate 16x9.5-inch Volk Racing TE37SL wheels (ET15 offset) wrapped in Toyo Open Country M/T tires (285/75R16). Front bumper replaced with a custom steel tube bumper (Bushwacker-style) with integrated Hella 500 driving lights and a Warn Zeon 10-S winch.

Rear bed delete with a custom aluminum flatbed (fabricated by Alu-Knight) featuring integrated side storage and a removable hardtop (FRP, painted in Mitsubishi Racing Green) with a pop-up sunroof (Webasto). Custom roll cage (Chassisworks 4-point) with integrated LED light bar mounts (Rigid Industries 50-inch E-Series) and a roof rack (Front Runner Slimline II).

Exterior accents include a custom carbon fiber hood (Seibon) with functional vents, a Mitsubishi Motors heritage badge (1980s JDM-style) on the grille, and a custom rear-mounted spare tire carrier (Swing-Away style, fabricated by Slee Off-Road). Paint scheme: Mitsubishi Racing Green with matte black accents (3M 1080 wrap) and JDM-style yellow turn signals (Depo Racing).
